design_philosophy.core_loop: Switch between four turtles to progress through side-scrolling and overhead exploration areas, with each turtle functioning as an extra life upon defeat
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource depletion (turtles as lives) combined with navigation and combat challenges across mixed level types
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control all four iconic TMNT brothers in a unified adventure, leveraging IP popularity and character switching mechanics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Single-player only design that treats four playable characters as glorified extra lives rather than simultaneous co-op; unbalanced difficulty philosophy; hybrid overhead exploration + side-scrolling combat structure not typical of contemporary beat-em-ups

difficulty.lives_system: Four turtles function as lives; turtle captured/defeated when health depleted; switching between turtles allows continuation
difficulty.checkpointing: Area-based progression structure; overhead areas must be explored to unlock side-scrolling sections
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can switch turtles at any time during gameplay, providing tactical choice and resource management of character availability

progression.unlock_mechanism: Exploration of overhead sections required to unlock side-scrolling combat stages; sequential area progression (Areas 1-4+)
replayability_hooks: Character-switching mechanics create variable playstyle options
Randomizer ROM hacks available indicating speedrun/challenge potential
Desire to improve execution across mixed level types

world_building_techniques: Character roster selection screen with visual differentiation
Regional narrative variation (Japanese version reimagines April as Splinter's daughter)

notable_bosses: Shredder (final boss, features unused flinching animations)
Rocksteady (unused flinching states suggest iterative design)
Area 3 boss (associated with captive Splinter narrative)

faction_indicators: Four distinct turtle characters with implied individual personalities
Splinter as captured ally requiring rescue/context
April O'Neil as supporting character (narrative role varies by region)
danger_signals: Foot Clan soldiers as recurring antagonists
environmental_storytelling: Urban New York settings grounding action in recognizable geography
Technodrome interior suggesting technological villain base
Overhead exploration areas contrasting with side-scrolling combat zones

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - primary design analysis source)
wikipedia (game series context and release information)
